company_overview.primary_theme: Strong iPhone and services sales despite tariff pressures

segment_highlights.driver: Customers rushed to purchase ahead of tariff increases

explicit_risks.risk: Competition in AI and smartphone market

causal_claims.claim: Strong iPhone sales are driven by customer anticipation of tariffs
causal_claims.evidence_reference: CEO Tim Cook's comments on earnings call

high_signal_summary: Apple reported Q3 2025 revenue of $89.04 billion, beating consensus by $4.04 billion.
EPS came in at $1.42, exceeding expectations by $0.02.
iPhone sales surged as customers rushed to buy ahead of tariff increases.
Services segment showed strong growth, contributing positively to overall performance.
CEO Tim Cook indicated a $1.1 billion impact from tariffs this quarter.
Management tone was improving, reflecting confidence in product demand.
Risks include ongoing tariff pressures and competition in AI.
Analysts remain bullish despite concerns over tariffs and market competition.
Stock rose 2.3% in after-hours trading following the earnings report.
Apple's strategic shift to India for iPhone production aims to mitigate tariff impacts.
Overall market sentiment remains cautious ahead of further economic data.
